response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine geospatial domain exposure (founder of the GISCollective geo-data platform, Green Map GIS work, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems work in DLang handling millions of requests per second. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust) in commercial settings, and his C++ experience is brief and dated (~7 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, plus a thesis project), with no Rust. He also has no direct routing, tiling, or navigation-guidance pipeline experience, so he would be a bet on transferable systems skills rather than a direct fit.
strengths: Real geospatial experience: founded and operates an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map
Low-latency systems background in DLang (async I/O, fibers, caching) at millions of requests/sec scale
Broad, adaptable engineer with strong testing/craftsmanship culture and Linux, Docker, Kubernetes experience
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014-2015); no Rust experience listed
No navigation, routing, or map-tile pipeline experience — geospatial work was web GIS mapping, not routing data
Recent years (2020-present) are web/full-stack TypeScript/Node.js product work, far from systems-level data pipelines
